episode_briefs.description: One entry per episode. Count must equal quick_reference.episode_count. Mirrors [EPISODE BRIEF] blocks in screenplay_markdown. Must align with outline episode_outlines for the same episode_id.

plot_summary.description: 2-4 sentences: what happens this episode and what advances.

opening_hook.description: One sentence: what retains the audience at episode open (conflict, information, visual tension).

cold_open_focus.description: One sentence: what must appear on screen in the first seconds to hold retention; aligns with first scene opening.

closing_hook.description: One sentence: unresolved question or crisis left for the next episode; must match final scene [SCENE ENDING].

core_satisfaction_beat.description: One sentence: primary satisfaction payoff type + landing; align with outline satisfaction_beat for this episode.

target_emotion.description: One sentence: target audience emotion by episode end.

screenplay_markdown.description: Full screenplay text covering every episode. After each ## EPISODE XX, include [EPISODE BRIEF] with six labeled lines (Episode Plot, Opening Hook, Cold Open Focus, Closing Hook, Core Satisfaction, Target Emotion) before the first ### SCENE. Every scene: scene header (6 fields) + [OPENING STATE] + [MAIN BODY] in action blocks + [SCENE ENDING]. No episode may be replaced with a summary or placeholder.

scene_crosswalk.description: One entry per scene. Feeds directly into the storyboard scene_index. scene_id must match the SCENE heading in screenplay_markdown exactly.

scene_id.description: e.g. 01.01. Must match SCENE heading in screenplay_markdown.

time.description: Time of day for this scene.

slugline.description: e.g. INT. HALE CHARITY GALA - NIGHT

characters.description: Character names as they appear in the screenplay (display names).

core_event.description: One sentence: the central narrative event of this scene. Mirrors the scene header Core Event field.

scene_result.description: One sentence: how the scene ends / what changes. Mirrors the scene header Scene Result field.

hook_type.description: Episode-ending scenes: must use cliffhanger / reversal / emotional_peak / setup. Mid-episode scenes: none.

emotional_function.description: The emotional arc function of this scene within the episode.

character_ids.description: character_id values aligned with setting_bible and outline character_cards.

key_props.description: Optional. prop_id values for props that play a narrative role in this scene. Used by storyboard to pre-identify prop shots.
